summary.summary: Adds explicit handling for NSPersistentHistoryTokenExpiredError in CoreData history fetching by catching that error, logging it, and calling a new recoverFromExpiredHistoryToken() routine. recoverFromExpiredHistoryToken() clears lastToken, retries fetchPersistentHistoryTransactionsAndChanges(), and on retry failure sets lastToken to the store's current persistent history token and calls viewContext.refreshAllObjects(). (diff:52b94a8f56a6) (commit:3c99ae4cfdd1) (file:93f04c40199f)
summary.userFacingImpact: Conservatively, this change attempts to restore the persistent-history-based change-merge pipeline when the persistent history token has expired by resetting the stored token and replaying available history; if replay fails it jumps to the current token and refreshes in-memory objects, which should prevent the app from silently stopping history-based updates in that error case. The patch notes that this error can happen after long inactivity or DB updates and that it was reproduced on a device, but does not quantify frequency or guarantee all scenarios are covered, so a human review of runtime behavior is recommended if needed. (pr:nightscout/Trio#1247) (commit:3c99ae4cfdd1) (pr:gordolio/Trio#1247)
